name: Deep Generative Model in Machine Learning: Theory, Principle and Efficacy (DeLTa 2026)

organizers.name: Sara Fridovich-Keil
organizers.affiliation: Georgia Tech (School of Electrical and Computer Engineering)

organizers.name: Andi Han
organizers.affiliation: University of Sydney (School of Mathematics and Statistics)

speakers.name: René Vidal
speakers.affiliation: University of Pennsylvania (Electrical and Systems Engineering)

summary: DeLTa 2026 is the premier theory-focused workshop on deep generative models at ICLR, now in its second edition after attracting 400+ attendees in 2025. Unites top researchers from statistics, optimization, and deep learning to address foundational questions about diffusion models, flow matching, VAEs, and autoregressive models. Speaker lineup (Doucet, Vidal, Steidl, Chen, Kadkhodaie) represents the top tier of generative model theorists globally — strong signal on which mathematical frameworks will shape production-scale diffusion systems in 2-3 years.

name: Latent & Implicit Thinking (LIT)
full_name: Latent & Implicit Thinking – Going Beyond CoT Reasoning
time_slot: 5:00 AM – 1:00 PM PDT (concurrent with World Models)

agenda.title: Panel: Key challenges in LLM reasoning in latent space

organizers.name: Zhiyuan Li
organizers.affiliation: Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago (TTIC) / University of Chicago

summary: LIT explores reasoning paradigms beyond Chain-of-Thought: continuous hidden representations, discrete reasoning tokens, looped/recurrent architectures, diffusion-based thinking, and non-autoregressive inference. Full schedule with 6 keynotes, 2 poster sessions, orals, and panel on 'latent space reasoning challenges.' Yuandong Tian (formerly Meta FAIR, now stealth startup) and Samy Bengio (Apple) are key speakers. CRITICAL CONFLICT: runs simultaneously with World Models workshop.

name: Scaling Post-training for LLMs (SPOT)

agenda.title: Panel: Prospects & Pitfalls of Scaling Post-Training of LLMs

organizers.name: Sewon Min
organizers.affiliation: UC Berkeley / Allen Institute for AI

organizers.name: Inderjit Dhillon
organizers.affiliation: Google / University of Texas at Austin

summary: SPOT develops a principled science of post-training for LLMs: SFT, RL-based alignment (RLHF/DPO), reward models, and inference-time scaling. 64 papers accepted. 9 invited speakers from Meta, Google, NVIDIA, Alibaba/Qwen, Prime Intellect, Reflection AI, Periodic Labs, Stanford, Berkeley. Panel: 'Prospects & Pitfalls of Scaling Post-Training.' IMPORTANT: Conflicts with FM4Science (both April 27).

name: AI Verification in the Wild (VerifAI-2)

agenda.note: Detailed timed schedule restricted to registered ICLR attendees. Special 2026 theme: 'Building verifiable tasks and environments for RL'

organizers.name: Sean Welleck
organizers.affiliation: Carnegie Mellon University, Language Technologies Institute

speakers.name: Gabriel Poesia
speakers.affiliation: Kempner Institute, Harvard University (incoming Asst Prof, U Michigan Fall 2026)

speakers.name: Federico Mora Rocha
speakers.affiliation: Amazon Web Services / incoming Asst Prof, University of Waterloo Fall 2026

speakers.name: Elizabeth Polgreen
speakers.affiliation: University of Edinburgh, School of Informatics

papers.title: GLEAN: Guideline-Grounded Evidence Accumulation for High-Stakes Agent Verification

summary: VerifAI-2 is the second iteration of AI Verification in the Wild, exploring the intersection of LLMs and formal verification (theorem provers, SAT solvers, execution monitoring). The 2026 special theme is 'Building verifiable tasks and environments for RL' — directly relevant to the verification/Lean investment bet. Speakers include Gabriel Poesia (Harvard Kempner, neurosymbolic AI), Naman Jain (Berkeley/Cursor, AI coding verification), and Armando Solar-Lezama (MIT, program synthesis pioneer).

name: World Models (2nd Workshop)
full_name: 2nd Workshop on World Models: Understanding, Modelling and Scaling

agenda.note: Full agenda not publicly available. Workshop includes 3 poster sessions and keynote talks.

summary: The 2nd World Models Workshop at ICLR 2026 (April 27, Rio). Building on the 2025 edition (1,500+ participants), explores scalable frameworks unifying generative modeling, sequential decision-making, multimodal learning, and causal reasoning. This edition adds systems-level sessions, robotics case studies, and failure-mode post-mortems. Keynotes: Schmidhuber (LSTM inventor), Sergey Levine (Physical Intelligence co-founder), Jiajun Wu (World Labs co-founder), Danijar Hafner (DreamerV3 creator), Mahmoud Assran (V-JEPA/JEPA). CRITICAL CONFLICT: runs simultaneously with Latent & Implicit Thinking workshop.

name: New Frontiers in Associative Memories (NFAM)

organizers.name: Benjamin Hoover
organizers.affiliation: Georgia Institute of Technology / IBM Research

organizers.name: Julia Kempe
organizers.affiliation: NYU Courant / joining Ellison Institute of Technology Oxford

organizers.name: Hilde Kuehne
organizers.affiliation: University of Tübingen (Tuebingen AI Center) / MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab

organizers.name: Zhaoyang Shi
organizers.affiliation: Harvard University, Department of Statistics (Postdoctoral Fellow)

speakers.name: Jay McClelland
speakers.affiliation: Stanford University (Lucie Stern Professor of Psychology)

speakers.name: Paul Liang
speakers.affiliation: MIT Media Lab / MIT EECS (Assistant Professor)

speakers.name: Natalia Berloff
speakers.affiliation: University of Cambridge, DAMTP (Professor of Applied Mathematics)

speakers.name: David Clark
speakers.affiliation: Kempner Institute, Harvard University (Research Fellow, theoretical neuroscientist)

speakers.name: Dmitry Krotov
speakers.affiliation: Independent Researcher (formerly MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab)

summary: Third iteration of New Frontiers in Associative Memories (following ICLR 2025 and NeurIPS 2023). Motivated by the Nobel Prize for Hopfield networks in 2024 and connections between associative memory and transformers/diffusion models. Full-day event with 6 invited talks, contributed talks, panel on 'Memory Can Improve LLMs,' and 3 poster sessions. Bridges optimization, deep learning, statistical physics, and neuroscience — key for understanding next-generation non-transformer architectures.

name: Foundation Models for Science (2nd Workshop)

organizers.name: Swarat Chaudhuri
organizers.affiliation: University of Texas at Austin / Google DeepMind

speakers.name: Michael Mahoney
speakers.affiliation: UC Berkeley / ICSI / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory

summary: 2nd FM4Science workshop focuses on problem-driven design of scientific foundation models covering astrophysics, biomedicine, earth science, materials science, PDEs, and quantum systems. Features $1,000 Best Paper prize (Renaissance Philanthropy) + NERSC compute credits. 7 invited speakers from UC Berkeley, UChicago, USC, UCSD, AWS. IMPORTANT: Conflicts with SPOT (both April 27).

name: Geometry-grounded Representation Learning and Generative Modeling (GRaM)

organizers.name: Sharvaree Vadgama
organizers.affiliation: UC San Diego / University of Amsterdam

summary: 2nd edition of GRaM workshop on geometry-grounded representation learning. 2026 theme: 'scale and simplicity' — when do geometric methods remain necessary vs. when simpler alternatives suffice? Topics: group equivariance, non-Euclidean generative models, differential geometry, loss landscape geometry. Papers published in PMLR. Invited speakers and agenda not yet publicly released.

name: ReALM-GEN: Real-World Constrained and Preference-Aligned Flow- and Diffusion-based Generative Models

organizers.name: Paris Giampouras
organizers.affiliation: University of Warwick (Assistant Professor, Computer Science)

speakers.name: Peter Holderrieth
speakers.affiliation: MIT CSAIL (PhD, advised by Tommi Jaakkola)

speakers.name: Michael Albergo
speakers.affiliation: Harvard Society of Fellows / MIT IAIFI (incoming Harvard Asst Prof Applied Math, July 2026)

summary: ReALM-GEN is the first ICLR workshop dedicated to post-training and inference-time alignment for diffusion and flow-based generative models — the critical commercial bottleneck as industry moves from training powerful base generative models to steering them toward preferences and constraints. Bridges theory (stochastic optimal control, Schrödinger bridges) with practice (reward fine-tuning, guidance, plug-and-play adapters) across image, video, molecules, and language. Panelists from NVIDIA (Vahdat, Kreis), Weizmann/DeepMind (Dekel), and Microsoft Research (Domingo-Enrich) represent essentially the leading commercial teams building production-grade controlled generation.

name: AI with Recursive Self-Improvement

speakers.name: Graham Neubig
speakers.affiliation: Carnegie Mellon University / All Hands AI

papers.title: Agent0: Unleashing Self-Evolving Agents from Zero Data via Tool-Integrated Reasoning
Contextual Drag: How Errors in the Context Affect LLM Reasoning
Learning to Continually Learn via Meta-learning Agentic Memory Designs
PostTrainBench: Can LLM Agents Automate LLM Post-Training?
SimpleMem: Efficient Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
Can Language Models Discover Scaling Laws?
Self-Improving World Models via Asymmetric Forward-Inverse Consistency
From Growing to Looping: A Unified View of Iterative Computation in LLMs
Self-Evolving Rubrics: Interpretable Instance-Level Criteria for Scalable RL
VLAW: Iterative Co-Improvement of Vision-Language-Action Policy and World Model
Inference-Time Scaling in Diffusion Models through Iterative Partial Refinement
Reasoning as Gradient: Scaling MLE Agents Beyond Tree Search
Actor-Curator: Scalable Policy-driven Curriculum Learning for RL Post-Training
GASP: Guided Asymmetric Self-Play For Coding LLMs

summary: World's first workshop dedicated exclusively to recursive self-improvement in AI. 110 accepted papers across 4 oral, 21 spotlight, 75 poster, 10 short formats. KEY PEOPLE: Chelsea Finn (Physical Intelligence co-founder, panelist), Graham Neubig (All Hands AI/OpenHands founder, speaker), Yuandong Tian (stealth startup, speaker), Jeff Clune (open-endedness researcher), Schmidhuber (organizer). Sponsors: Tencent, Meta. Expected 500+ attendees.

name: Test-Time Updates — 3rd Workshop (TTU)

organizers.name: Evan Shelhamer
organizers.affiliation: University of British Columbia / Vector Institute / CIFAR AI Chair

organizers.name: Xiaoxiao Li
organizers.affiliation: University of British Columbia / Vector Institute

speakers.name: Jonas Geiping
speakers.affiliation: ELLIS Institute Tübingen / Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems

summary: 3rd TTU workshop covering test-time adaptation (TTA), post-training model editing, in-context learning, and online continual learning. Two submission tracks: main (4-page) and tiny (2-page). Focus areas: stability-plasticity trade-offs, cost-aware and green TTU, foundation model editing. 6 invited speakers.

name: Agentic AI in the Wild: From Hallucinations to Reliable Autonomy

speakers.name: Florian Buettner
speakers.affiliation: Goethe-University Frankfurt and German Cancer Research Center

summary: An interdisciplinary workshop exploring hallucinations, uncertainty, and reliability challenges in large-scale agentic AI systems that operate autonomously without continuous human oversight.

name: Logical Reasoning of LLMs
summary: A workshop addressing the gap between LLMs' strong language capabilities and their limited logical reasoning, exploring deduction, induction, abduction, symbolic integration, and benchmark development.

name: Memory for LLM-Based Agentic Systems (MemAgents)
summary: A focused forum on the memory layer for LLM-based agentic systems, bridging researchers across reinforcement learning, memory research, LLMs, and neuroscience to advance episodic, semantic, and working memory architectures for long-horizon agents.

name: Multi-Agent Learning in Generative AI Era

organizers.name: Caroline Wang
organizers.affiliation: University of Texas at Austin / Google DeepMind

speakers.name: Peter Stone
speakers.affiliation: University of Texas at Austin / Sony AI

summary: A workshop exploring the convergence of multi-agent learning and generative AI across LLM-based agent coordination, real-world distributed system control, and human-AI interaction.
